mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in real retail sales captures consumer spending momentum that translates into higher corporate revenue expectations, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC between month-over-month RSAFS growth and 20-day SPX forward returns falls below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive returns occur in fewer than 55% of months with rising RSAFS.

mechanism_narrative: Once the federal funds rate exceeds 0.25%, monetary tightening reduces market liquidity and raises discount rates applied to future corporate cash flows, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: After FEDFUNDS crosses above 0.25%, the subsequent 20-day SPX return is positive in more than 50% of instances, or the mean return is not significantly negative (one-sided t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent monthly increases in industrial production and nonfarm payrolls signal a broad-based expansion in both output and employment, and their joint occurrence predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days beyond either signal alone.
failure_condition: Months where both INDPRO and PAYEMS increase do not produce a significantly higher average 20-day SPX return than months where only one increases (difference-of-means t-test p > 0.05), or the joint signal Rank IC is below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion with unemployment below 5.5%, additional payroll gains raise wage pressure concerns and tighten monetary policy expectations, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days despite the headline labor strength.
failure_condition: During periods with UNRATE below 5.5%, positive month-over-month PAYEMS changes are followed by positive 60-day SPX returns in more than 55% of cases, or the mean return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production grows faster than retail sales, unintended inventory accumulation signals future production cutbacks and margin pressure, predicting negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Periods with INDPRO monthly change exceeding RSAFS monthly change are not followed by negative 60-day SPX returns (mean return t-test p > 0.05), or the relative-growth signal Rank IC is above -0.02.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in housing starts reflects strengthening residential investment and construction activity, which immediately lifts cyclical sectors and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive month-over-month changes in HOUST coincide with negative 5-day SPX returns in more than 50% of observations, or the mean 5-day return is not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A three-month decline in the unemployment rate signals accelerating household income growth and consumer confidence, which re-rates earnings expectations and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Three-month declines in UNRATE are followed by negative 60-day SPX returns in more than 50% of episodes, or the Rank IC between UNRATE change and 60-day returns is below 0.02 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in CPIAUCSL exceeding 0.3% signals demand-driven inflation that raises nominal discount rate expectations and tightens financial conditions, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Months where CPIAUCSL increases by more than 0.3% are followed by positive 5-day SPX returns in more than 50% of instances, or the mean return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).
